evidence: Each frame carries a single load-bearing element: frame 2 the three probe stills, frame 3 the lilac dial, frame 4 the aha sentence over the worked chip, frames 5-6 the a·b annotation truing up next to the numerals.
No frame introduces more than four novel elements at once; frame 2's three labeled probe stills and the later single dial plus one annotation stay within budget.
Labels sit on their objects: 'candidate'/'probe' beside their arrows, 'along/across/against' under each still, 'oppose/ignore/agree' on the dial rim, and 'a · b' beside the numerals.
The 'The dot product is a similarity meter.' line is a short key-term heading sitting directly above the visible worked equation it annotates.
All labels, scores, and the equation numerals are rendered at comfortably readable size relative to frame height.
Every focal element sits inside the frame with margin; nothing is cropped by an edge.
No text or symbol overlaps another; the a·b annotation, the numeral chip, and the dial occupy separate regions.
Colors stay consistent: candidate periwinkle and probe amber across stills, lilac for the a·b/parameter annotation and scores, mint for the converged aha line, amber for the needle.
Every element—arrows, scores, dial, needle, annotation—carries propositional content; no decorative filler is present.

The intent is delivered: three scored probe stills (frame 2) collapse into a lilac dial with needle over oppose/ignore/agree (frame 3), the mint aha sentence trues up (frame 4), and lilac 'a · b' anchors next to the (2,1)·(1,3)=5 numerals (frames 5-6).
The a·b annotation sits immediately left of the numerals it names and the dial rests just below the worked equation.
Across frames the stills build into the dial and the annotation trues up incrementally rather than materializing whole.
The compositions keep generous negative space with a clear top-to-bottom reading order from heading to equation to dial.
